entry.title: Washington Post: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Fears of ‘large-scale offensive’ in Luhansk as Russia advances
  • Canada banning single-use plastics to combat pollution, climate change
  • Iraq’s wildcard cleric upends politics as summer’s heat descends
  • In Venezuela, priests convicted of abuse have returned to ministry
  • As businesses flee Russia, a New York model returns to Moscow
  • World’s heaviest freshwater fish, a 661-pound stingray, caught in Cambodia
  • How Kaliningrad, Russian territory surrounded by NATO, is tangled in Ukraine war
  • Kremlin says captured Americans are ‘soldiers of fortune,’ not subject to Geneva Conventions
  • Japan has always been refugee-averse. Then Ukraine happened.
  • Ben Stiller tells Zelensky in Ukraine: ‘You’re my hero’
  • George Lamming, renowned Caribbean novelist, dies at 94
  • As Latin America embraces a new left, the U.S. could take a back seat
  • Second American is reported killed in Ukraine fighting
  • Filipino government lawyer fatally shot while vacationing in U.S.
  • Hong Kong’s landmark Jumbo floating restaurant sinks at sea
  • Britain will electronically tag some asylum seekers with GPS devices
  • Spain devastated by wildfires amid record-breaking heat wave
  • Millions stranded as heavy flooding sweeps through Bangladesh and India
  • Israel’s leader to dissolve Knesset, triggering new elections
  • Former journalist among five British men released from Taliban custody
  • UN biodiversity summit moved from China over COVID-19 policy
  • S. Korea runs 1st successful homegrown space rocket launch
  • Two women still hospitalized after brutal attack in China
  • Dozens dead, thousands homeless in Bangladesh, India floods
  • 2 stabbed in Sweden, 1 in life-threatening condition
  • Speaker at meeting of Ginni Thomas group called Biden’s win illegitimate long after Jan. 6, video shows
  • Wildfire in New Jersey may become largest in 15 years, officials say
  • Investors paid thousands for rare wines. It was a scam, feds say.
  • Held at gunpoint behind the wheel, a driver sped to alert officers
  • Louisville mayor punched by man in second attack on local Democrats
  • Jan. 6 hearing live updates: Panel to hear testimony on Trump campaign to pressure state officials
  • Crypto’s frozen mystery: The fate of billions in Celsius deposits
  • Israel is hoping its gas will finally make it part of the Middle East
  • What your future office could look like — if you even need to be there
  • Will Elizabeth Holmes’s former business partner face the same fate?
  • Cordless power tools are the rage but may cost you more in the end
  • Remote work continues to shift U.S. housing landscape, Zillow finds
  • How to hire a reliable roofing contractor for your home
  • Why This Bear Market Is Bad for Stocks and Bonds
  • Why a $33 Billion SPAC Deal Couldn’t Pay Its Bankers
  • Tech’s $1 Billion Unicorns Eclipsed by Centaurs
  • Mortgage Lenders Timed the Market Perfectly
  • Why the ECB Needs New Tools for Bond ‘Fragmentation’
  • How China’s Property Developers Got Into Such a Mess
  • Why China and US Disagree on Forced Labor in Xinjiang
  • How an Emboldened Far-Right Is Changing French Politics
  • The most memorable lines from the Jan. 6 hearings so far
  • The courts still haven’t figured out how to reconcile science with law
  • The Pentagon gets more money, and Americans pay the price
  • Republicans can’t celebrate ‘Top Gun: Maverick’ while denigrating the military
  • Robin Thede on comedically reflecting on Black America through ‘A Black Lady Sketch Show’
  • Pediatric covid vaccines could change how we deal with the pandemic
  • How the GOP is making national policy one state at a time
  • A generation of homeowners encounters a strange new market
  • Be careful about lionizing Jan. 6 witnesses. They failed to stop the insurrection.
  • Finally, ‘Top Gun’ has a female pilot. Real life should catch up.
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • U.S. Officials Predict Path of War in Ukraine
  • In Ukraine, One Woman’s Shopping Trip Over the Last Bridge in Lysychansk
  • Russia’s Blockade of Ukraine Is ‘War Crime,’ Top E.U. Official Says

  • In Ad, Shotgun-Toting Greitens Asks Voters to Go ‘RINO Hunting’
  • New Mexico’s Largest Wildfire Was a Prescribed Burn. What Are Victims Owed?
  • What We Do — and, Frustratingly, Don’t — Know About Long Covid
  • Feminism Made a Faustian Bargain With Celebrity Culture. Now It’s Paying the Price.
  • Mike Pence Was of Two Minds
  • If I Get Canceled, I Want Friends, Not Allies
  • How Animals See Themselves
  • Everyone Deserves an Equal Chance for a Campsite

  • Gustavo Petro Promised to Transform Colombia as President. Can He Do It?

  • New Brand of Activist Takes Aim at Ukraine War and Climate Crisis, Together
  • China’s Expanding Surveillance State: Takeaways From a NYT Investigation

  • ‘We Are Not Safe’: Ethiopians Flee Massacre That Killed Hundreds
  • The Killing of Shireen Abu Akleh: Tracing a Bullet to an Israeli Convoy
  • Ruinous Flooding in India and Bangladesh Kills at Least 116
  • 132 Civilians Killed by Rebel Attacks in Central Mali, Government Says
  • Russian Journalist’s Nobel Medal Sells for $103.5 Million
  • Jumbo, Hong Kong’s Floating Restaurant, Sinks After Capsizing
  • Australian Judge Delays Brittany Higgins Rape Trial
  • Chinese Omicron Study Renews Debate Over ‘Zero Covid’ Policy
  • Finding Traces of Harriet Tubman on Maryland’s Eastern Shore
  • 1972: A 36-Day Hunger Strike Ends in Northern Ireland
  • Young U.S. Children Will Start Getting Vaccines, Though Hurdles Remain
  • Libyans Try to Move On From Conflict With Comedy and Burgers
  • Your Tuesday Briefing
  • Kremlin Calls Captured Americans ‘Soldiers of Fortune’
  • Failed Autopsies, False Arrests: A Risk of Bias in Death Examinations
  • In a Town Crippled by Grief, the Healing Power of a Perfect Pitch
  • Expert Panel to Call for Overhaul of U.S. Public Health System
  • In a Return to the Land, Tribes Will Jointly Manage a National Monument
  • Clela Rorex, Clerk Who Broke a Gay-Marriage Barrier, Dies at 78
  • Biden Says He Is Considering Seeking a Gas Tax Holiday
  • Dr. Paul M. Ellwood Jr., Architect of the H.M.O., Is Dead at 95
  • Parts of Yellowstone Will Reopen This Week After Damaging Floods
  • Dutch Government Activates ‘Early Warning’ Because of Russian Cutbacks on Gas
  • Can Natural Gas Be Used to Create Power With Fewer Emissions?
  • Red Flags for Forced Labor Found in China’s Car Battery Supply Chain
  • When Customers Say Their Money Was Stolen on Zelle, Banks Often Refuse to Pay
  • Google Says It’s Time for Longtime Small-Business Users to Pay Up
  • How a Religious Sect Landed Google in a Lawsuit
News from the New York Times

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • FIFA move to protect foreign players affected by Ukraine conflict
  • Airlines may temper capacity plans over staff shortages
  • Ukraine on course for EU candidacy at summit, EU says
  • Volunteer archaeologists discover ancient Roman temple in Netherlands
  • Malaysia to scrap subsidies for some cooking oil products
  • South China provinces raise alerts as flooding breaks records
  • Grace days, more academic support among recommendations in report on undergraduates' mental health

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 21 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-21 11:02AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong protests: 2 teenagers sentenced to correctional training, hard labour for vandalising bakery during 2019 protests
  • President Xi Jinping’s ‘deep feelings’ for Hong Kong highlighted by state media as city prepares to mark 25th anniversary of handover
  • Hong Kong and BN(O) passport holders can travel to South Korea visa-free again after restrictions eased, while city logs 1,198 Covid cases
  • Will NFT, metaverse developments in Hong Kong help deliver the city’s next ‘Octopus moment’ in a post-pandemic world?
  • Before the storm (2007-2012): Hong Kong’s national pride and a rare political deal in 25 photos
  • Former Hong Kong leader CY Leung says American Express apologised to wife Regina after suing her over allegedly unpaid HK$93,155 credit card bill
  • Satire in Hong Kong: ‘You have to laugh, or you will cry,’ says political cartoonist Harry Harrison
  • Anti-terrorism campaign urges Hong Kong hardware store owners, chemical suppliers to report suspicious customers
  • Ex-Hong Kong governor: China breached city autonomy pledge ‘comprehensively’
  • Hong Kong Palace Museum to act as ‘window’ for promoting Chinese culture, director of Beijing counterpart says
  • Hong Kong’s iconic Jumbo Floating Restaurant sinks in South China Sea after encountering adverse conditions
  • Travel disruption to spill into first quarter of 2023, head of global airline association warns amid labour shortage and rising demand
  • Hong Kong could start quarantine-free business travel corridor with mainland China, fight for more flexible rules on border reopening, advisers urge
  • 1 in 10 Hong Kong kindergartens warn of possible closure in coming years as enrolment declines
  • Hong Kong government to give eligible elderly receiving allowances an extra HK$995 a month from September 1
  • Hong Kong Bar Association head stresses importance of ‘public perception’ after incoming deputy justice minister retains party membership
  • Debate over whether Hong Kong was indeed British colony reflects lack of education on Chinese constitution, lawmakers say
  • Hong Kong film star Louis Koo sees G Storm poster image misappropriated by Chinese NFT platform
  • ‘Spicy policies’ helped fortify Hong Kong’s market, save homeowners from negative-equity trap, defaults, ex-HKMA CEO Chan says
  • Qatar World Cup organisers reverse course on Taiwan name change
  • Does China’s demotion of its deputy foreign minister signal a rethink over Russia ties?
  • China’s southern provinces on alert as flooding breaks records while heat hits further north
  • Tencent’s WeChat bans public accounts from providing secondary trading services for NFTs
  • ‘We will not give up’: wife of jailed Chinese coal magnate vows to push for new trial after he loses appeal
  • COP15: China gives up host role with call for urgent meeting on biodiversity, UN says
  • Tencent trims stake in Koolearn, New Oriental’s live-streaming unit, as new venture fuels a six-fold jump in stock
  • EU companies in China pay the price for Brussels stance on Xinjiang
  • NetEase to launch app of virtual meeting platform Yaotai in August, as competition heats up on metaverse-related products
  • Teen girl in China steals US$18,000 meant for sick mother’s operation to go on a shopping spree
  • China’s political party school in Africa takes first students from 6 countries
  • China’s Horn of Africa envoy tells regional peace conference he is ready to mediate disputes
  • European nations tell China ‘self-isolation’ is stoking ‘mistrust’ in plea for easing of Covid-19 curbs
  • Chinese fugitive wanted for racist videos of Malawi children arrested in Zambia
  • China’s Mars mission on track to lead the world in retrieving Martian rocks by 2031, says programme veteran
  • Mass evacuations as record rain, floods strike southern China
  • Cop 15: UN Biodiversity Conference set to move from China to Canada after Covid-19 delays
  • China’s anti-corruption drive brings tighter rules for officials’ families to stop them exploiting their connections
  • China’s Russian oil imports surged over 50 per cent in May, boosted by discounts amid Ukraine war sanctions
  • China says latest anti-ballistic missile test ‘achieved objective’
  • US and Chinese businesses should team up to tackle climate change, says former US treasury secretary Paulson
  • South Korea successfully launches home-grown Nuri space rocket after failed attempt last year
  • Singapore unveils US$1 billion package as cost of living rises amid inflation, Ukraine war
  • Bangladesh, India scramble to help 9 million people hit by ‘worst floods in 122 years’
  • As BTS ‘takes a break’, what does it mean for the K-pop supergroup’s future?
  • Singapore seeks ‘online safety’ rules to remove or block harmful social media content
  • Coronavirus: Is North Korea getting ready to declare victory over Covid-19?
  • A new Quad? Japan, South Korea, Australia, New Zealand eye four-way anti-China summit on sidelines of Nato meet
  • Indonesian Islamist group Khilafatul Muslimin with ‘hundreds of thousands’ of members wants to build caliphate
  • World’s biggest freshwater fish caught by Cambodian in the Mekong River
  • Japan court upholds gay marriage ban, saying it is constitutional
  • Rice job: Philippines’ Bongbong Marcos appoints himself agriculture chief, plans ‘impossible’ rice price slash
  • Airlines restoring flights faster than planned to meet demand
  • UK grinds to halt as biggest rail strike in 30 years strands thousands of commuters
  • Inside Ukraine’s secret, helicopter rescue missions: ‘We lost a lot of pilots’
  • Russia’s Putin alert for signs of eroding Western unity on Ukraine
  • Belgium returns Congo independence hero Patrice Lumumba’s tooth to family
  • Elon Musk’s 18-year-old trans child seeks name change to sever ties with father
  • As Russia closes its doors on academics, Central Asian universities have an opportunity to expand their networks
  • Despite Western weapons, Ukraine is outgunned in the east
  • Ukraine war: US and allies mulling Russian oil price cap, Treasury chief Janet Yellen says
  • Ukraine war: Russia says captured US fighters must be ‘held responsible for their crimes’
  • Hopes fade for swift Nato accession for Finland and Sweden
  • Israel facing fifth election in three years after fragile coalition collapses
  • Hollywood star Ben Stiller visiting Ukraine says ‘seeking safety is a right’
  • Russian journalist sells Nobel Peace Prize medal for record US$103.5 million to aid Ukrainian children
  • Evergrande: defaulted developer gets delisting reminder as restructuring, probe on US$2 billion deposits leave investors in the dark
  • China’s climate policies are forcing renewable energy developers to incorporate costly storage facilities into new solar and wind projects
  • US Federal Reserve’s attempt to turn inflation tide leaves Asian economies exposed
  • Buying UK property: here’s a tactic to get faster mortgage loan approval to fund your purchase
  • Hong Kong stocks log longest winning streak in three weeks on China recovery optimism as property and casino stocks rally
  • Kowloon may attract more Chinese firms as office-rental cost gap narrows between Greater Bay Area cities and Hong Kong
  • Hong Kong developers lower prices to generate sales amid interest rate hikes, high supply
  • New Zealand’s housing reforms lead the way on tackling affordability and speculation
  • China’s national ambitions have become untethered from personal aspirations in the name of zero Covid
  • Fear grips cryptocurrency traders and magnifies the global rout in US$247 billion DeFi market, Binance’s founder CZ says
  • British MPs call for Wimbledon to drop HSBC as a sponsor over endorsement of Hong Kong’s national security law
  • Chinese game studio XD makes US a priority in its overseas expansion drive as home market becomes increasingly difficult to navigate
  • Chinese court rules car sale made using digital currency invalid as Beijing’s unrelenting crypto crackdown continues
  • Hong Kong Philharmonic thrill with vibrant Respighi and a pyrotechnic piccolo premiere
  • Documenta 2022: curated by Asians for first time, German art event’s highs and lows as opening week kicks off
  • Broker: Hirokazu Kore-eda, acclaimed Japanese director, on his first Korean film, starring Song Kang-ho, Gang Dong-won and IU
  • Netflix K-drama Alchemy of Souls: Jung So-min, Lee Jae-wook in CGI-heavy fantasy action romance that offers a magical mix of genres
  • Why is mead making a comeback? The craft brewers in Hong Kong reviving ancient alcoholic beverage for the modern drinker
  • Mamma Mia! ABBA’s avatar concerts highlight change as performing arts innovation connects with audiences
  • When Hong Kong seamen returned from the Falklands war in 1982, including survivors of air attacks on their ships
  • Why use of social media to market fashion in China and West is so different – the hard sell from influencers doesn’t work in US or Europe … yet
  • Taboo: how the word came to describe topics such as menstruation and race, and how it helped one tourist hotspot achieve zero Covid
  • What do we see when dying? How a psychedelic trip can trigger at life’s end, and why we need to be better prepared for it
  • Jongga, No. 1 kimchi brand in Korea since 1987, innovates the country’s essential food worldwide
  • Takashi Murakami’s NFT apology to fans: launching just before the 2022 crypto market crash, the Japanese contemporary artist saw his Murakami Flowers’ value drop from US$260,000 to a mere US$2,000
  • Milan Men’s Fashion Week: Prada blended grunge with nostalgia for spring/summer 2023, with Miuccia Prada and Raf Simons creating suits, sleeveless short sets and trenches for the season
  • Inside Bollywood legend Jackie Shroff’s US$4 million Mumbai family home: he lives in a luxurious eight-bedroom flat with Tiger, Krishna and Ayesha – and counts Rani Mukerji as a neighbour
  • Inside tech billionaire Larry Ellison’s US$160 million superyacht Musashi: the Oracle co-founder’s 88-metre, Japanese-style vessel was even featured on Netflix’s The Dropout
  • 9 of the most famous celebrity twin pairs in Hollywood, from Mary-Kate and Ashley Olsen, and Brangelina’s children Vivienne and Knox Jolie-Pitt, to Khloé Kardashian’s besties Malika and Khadijah Haqq
  • Meet Dylan and Paris Brosnan, Pierce Brosnan’s hunky sons: both are fashion models, working with the likes of Moschino and Karlie Kloss, and were Golden Globes ambassadors in 2020
  • Who are the richest Hunger Games stars? Net worths, ranked – from Oscar winner Jennifer Lawrence and OG actor Woody Harrelson, to rock legend Lenny Kravitz and hunky Hemsworth brother Liam
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Staff at crypto operator Terraform Labs hit with flight ban

entry.title: Guardian: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Nigel Slater’s recipe for courgettes with gorgonzola sauce
  • 4thWrite short story prize seeks best new writers of colour

  • Boris Johnson suggests he is digging in on rail strikes, telling cabinet they must ‘stay the course’ – live
  • Shadow ministers urge Starmer to start picking new candidate for Corbyn’s seat
  • Average UK food bill rises by £380 a year as grocery inflation hits 13-year high
  • Beyoncé: Break My Soul review – house anthem doesn’t break the mould

  • Wimbledon hoping big data will improve fan experience

  • Notes from a tranquil island: slow walking in Orkney

  • Visa delays leave UK families with adopted babies stranded in Pakistan
  • ‘We make nature here’: pioneering Dutch project repairs image after outcry over starving animals
  • ‘Skinny, bendy and blonde’: women of colour challenge racism in UK yoga
  • US ban on cotton from forced Uyghur labour comes into force
  • ‘Twiddle a knob and make a face’ – Norman Cook on his mental health DJ classes
  • Life will find a way: could scientists make Jurassic Park a reality?
  • UK lawyers gather evidence for action against countries over Yazidi genocide
  • ‘We were just rowdy 16-year-olds’: how a police caution blighted a career
  • Texas school shooting: heavily armed police with ballistic shields were there ‘within 19 minutes’

  • Shadowy Strava users spy on Israeli military with fake routes in bases

  • How Matt Fitzpatrick found the extra length required for US Open glory | Ewan Murray

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
